name: Love in Disguise
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Milk, to beat with egg
recipeInstructions:
You may get 2 or 3 large hearts for this weight or several smaller ones.
Whatever the size, they will be slashed; this is normal. Wash the hearts
and trim away waste - fat, membrane, gristle and arteries. Soak in lightly
salted water for 10 minutes then rinse, drain and dry well. Chop the onion
finely and soften it in 1 oz butter. Away from the heat stir in the
breadcrumbs, lemon zest, parsley, salt, and pepper. Bind with the egg and milk.
Use this mixture to stuff the heart cavities, securing the openings with toothpicks or cocktail sticks; perfect closures are not necessary.
Whizz the canned tomatoes in a blender, stir in the vinegar, and add enough stock to make up to 1 pint. Thoroughly dust the hearts with well-seasoned flour. Melt 1 oz butter in a flameproof casserole, stir in the leftover flour until it browns slightly. Blend in the tomato mixture to create a smooth, bubbling hot sauce. Lay the prepared hearts in the sauce, tuck the bay leaves among them, cover with greaseproof paper and the lid, and cook at 325°F (160°C) for 1 hour.
Gently turn the hearts and continue cooking for an additional 1 to 1-1/2 hours until the meat is tender. Transfer the hearts to a warmed serving dish. Stir in the mustard, add salt and pepper to taste, and carefully blend in the yoghurt if desired. Pour the sauce over the hearts, scatter with chopped parsley, and serve with boiled potatoes or noodles.
